# Welcome to the Observability Workbench Community

**Category: Announcements (pin this discussion)**

---

Welcome to the Observability Workbench community. We are glad you are here.

## What is Observability Workbench?

Observability Workbench is an open-source, Fabric-native observability tool that solves critical monitoring gaps in Microsoft Fabric:

- **Long-retention monitoring** -- break free from the 30-day monitoring hub limit with 90-day or 365-day event retention in your own Eventhouse
- **Cross-item correlation** -- trace failures across pipelines, notebooks, dataflows, and semantic model refreshes with automatic correlation IDs
- **SLO tracking** -- define Service Level Objectives for data freshness, pipeline success rates, and execution duration, then track them over time
- **Proactive alerting** -- get notified before SLOs breach, not after users complain about stale reports
- **Incident timelines** -- see the full story of what happened, across all Fabric items, in a single view

## How to get started

1. **Star the repo**: [github.com/tenfingerseddy/FabricWorkloads](https://github.com/tenfingerseddy/FabricWorkloads) -- helps others discover the project
2. **Install**: `npm install @kane-ai/observability-workbench`
3. **Quick health check**: `npx fabric-health-check` -- get an instant workspace health report
4. **Read the docs**: Check the README for setup instructions and the KQL community query pack

## How to use Discussions

We have set up several discussion categories to keep conversations organized:

| Category | Use it for |
|----------|-----------|
| **Announcements** | Release notes, roadmap updates, community news (maintainers only) |
| **Q&A** | Questions about setup, configuration, KQL queries, troubleshooting |
| **Ideas** | Feature suggestions, brainstorming, "what if we could..." discussions |
| **Show and Tell** | Share your setup, dashboards, custom KQL queries, SLO configurations |
| **KQL Queries** | Share and discover KQL queries for Fabric observability |
| **General** | Everything else -- introductions, off-topic chat, meta discussions |

## Community guidelines

- **Be helpful and respectful.** We are all here to make Fabric observability better.
- **Search before posting.** Your question may already have an answer.
- **Share context.** When asking for help, include your Fabric capacity SKU, region, and relevant error output.
- **Never post secrets.** Redact tokens, client secrets, tenant IDs, and connection strings before sharing.
- **Give back.** If someone helped you, consider sharing what you learned with the next person.

## How to contribute

We welcome contributions of all kinds:

- **KQL queries**: Share useful monitoring queries via the KQL Queries discussion category or submit a PR to the community query pack
- **SLO templates**: Have a good SLO definition? Share it so others can benefit
- **Bug reports**: Found something broken? Open an issue with reproduction steps
- **Feature requests**: Have an idea? Start a discussion in Ideas, then we can convert it to a tracked issue
- **Code contributions**: Check the issues labeled `good-first-issue` for a starting point
- **Documentation**: Typos, unclear explanations, missing examples -- all improvements welcome

## Stay connected

- **GitHub**: Star and watch the repo for release notifications
- **Discussions**: You are already here
- **Issues**: [Track bugs and features](https://github.com/tenfingerseddy/FabricWorkloads/issues)

We are building this in the open and your feedback directly shapes the roadmap. Every issue, discussion, and contribution matters.

Welcome aboard.
